name: Las Cuevas de la Araña — Tour from Bicorp
name_original: Las Cuevas de la Araña — Tour desde Bicorp
region_intro: Hiking around Bicorp offers diverse landscapes characterized by rounded hills, dense pine forests, and abundant crystalline water features. The region features impressive gorges carved by rivers like the Fraile, as well as significant ravines. Hikers can explore a network of paths that traverse these natural environments, providing varied terrain and elevation. Best hiking trails around Bicorp The most popular hiking route is Circular walk through the Charcos de Quesa, a 5.4 miles (8.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete, offering a popular option suitable for various fitness levels. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Cuevas de la Araña — Tour from Bicorp, a difficult 12.8 miles (20.6 km) path leading to a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its ancient rock art. Local hikers also love the Fraile Pool – Camping Los Botijos loop from Las Arenas, a 9.8 miles (15.8 km) trail leading through the River Fraile canyon, often completed in about 4 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Bicorp is defined by rounded hills, pine forests, and crystalline water features, including gorges and ravines. The network of routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Bicorp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Bicorp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Bicorp?
A: Bicorp offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking routes, ranging from easy strolls to challenging treks. This includes 6 easy, 30 moderate, and 3 difficult trails, ensuring options for every experience level.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes around Bicorp?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, characterized by impressive rounded hills, dense pine forests, and abundant crystalline water features. You'll encounter stunning gorges carved by rivers like the Fraile, picturesque waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and deep pools perfect for wild swimming. Geological wonders like the Gola de Lucino Gorge also await exploration.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Bicorp?
A: Yes, Bicorp has several routes suitable for families. The area features paths of varying lengths and difficulties, including 6 easy trails. A popular choice for a pleasant outing is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818068753/circular-walk-through-the-charcos-de-quesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular walk through the Charcos de Quesa&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.4-mile (8.7 km) trail that takes around 2 hours 39 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Bicorp?
A: Yes, many of Bicorp's trails are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e818068753/circular-walk-through-the-charcos-de-quesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular walk through the Charcos de Quesa&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31434416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fraile Pool – Camping Los Botijos loop from Las Arenas&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the River Fraile canyon.

Q: What historical or cultural landmarks can I visit while hiking in Bicorp?
A: Bicorp is home to significant historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822798768/las-cuevas-de-la-arana-tour-from-bicorp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cuevas de la Araña&lt;/a&gt; (Spider Caves) are a UNESCO World Heritage Site, famous for ancient rock art including a rare depiction of a honey-gatherer. You can also explore the Barranco Moreno Ravine, which hosts other rock art sites, and the 17th-century Las Pedrizas Fort.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or refreshing dips along the trails?
A: Absolutely. Bicorp is known for its crystalline water features. The River Fraile offers deep pools and the opportunity for walking directly in the river. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Corbinet Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; has a recreational area where swimming is permitted, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5825165" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural pool on the Sellent River in Bolbaite&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent spot for a refreshing dip.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Bicorp?
A: The hiking trails in Bicorp are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the refreshing water features found along the routes.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trailheads in Bicorp?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead vary, Bicorp is generally accessible. Many routes, especially those starting near the town center or recreational areas like Benefetal and River Fraile, offer convenient parking options. It's advisable to check the route details on komoot for specific parking information for your chosen trail.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic views of the Bicorp region?
A: Yes, Bicorp offers several scenic viewpoints. Along the Barranco Moreno Ravine, viewpoints like La Piñeta and El Olmiquio provide expansive overlooks of the area. Additionally, ascending the road towards the sports center from El Raval offers a beautiful perspective of the meander and Bicorp town center, with the vegetable plots of La Rambla in the foreground.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bicorp?
A: Bicorp's Mediterranean climate makes it suitable for hiking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for exploring the trails. Summers can be warm, but the abundance of water features like rivers and waterfalls provides opportunities for refreshing breaks. Winter hiking is also possible, with milder temperatures than many other regions.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Bicorp?
A: Yes, Bicorp offers challenging terrain for experienced hikers. The region includes 3 difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e822798768/las-cuevas-de-la-arana-tour-from-bicorp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cuevas de la Araña — Tour from Bicorp&lt;/a&gt;, a 12.8-mile (20.6 km) path. Pico Caroche (Caroig), reaching 1,126 meters, also provides more demanding routes and expansive views.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Bicorp?
A: Many natural areas in Spain, including those around Bicorp, are generally dog-friendly, especially on marked trails. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife. Check local signage or specific trail regulations for any restrictions, particularly around sensitive historical sites or recreational swimming areas.
tips: It is a set of caves dating between 9000 and 1400 BC. They were discovered at the beginning of the 20th century and declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1998 and contain one of the most famous representations in the world, the Bicorp man climbing lianas to obtain honey from wild bees.
